data.title: NEFI Applauds ASTM Heating Oil Specification For Up To 50% Biodiesel Blends
data.description: On April 23, 2026, in Washington, the National Energy & Fuels Institute (NEFI) expressed approval for the ASTM's update of fuel oils specification ASTM D396. This revision now encompasses grades for fuel blends with 21% to 50% biodiesel content. This change directly impacts retail heating fuel dealers in the Northeastern region, providing them with greater flexibility in offering environmentally friendly fuel options.

data.title: In HelloNation, Painting Expert Shaun Taddia of Brookhaven, Explains How to Select a Long-Lasting Ceramic Finish for Modern Homes
data.description: In Brookhaven, Georgia on April 23, 2026, an article from HelloNation discusses the selection of a durable ceramic finish for homeowners. The piece highlights insights from Painting Expert Shaun Taddia from Rhino Shield by Georgia Coatings, Inc. It delves into the qualities that make ceramic finishes long-lasting and the factors that homeowners should consider when making this choice.

data.title: Medpace Flags Rising Cancellations, Near-Term Growth Uncertain
data.description: Medpace Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: MEDP) stock is experiencing a decline following the release of its first-quarter earnings report. CEO August Troendle revealed a significant increase in cancellations during the current quarter, particularly in oncology and cardiovascular sectors, resulting in the highest backlog cancellations in over a year. This surge in cancellations is causing immediate revenue pressures, with sequential growth expected to remain flat for the near future. While year-over-year growth is anticipated to be maintained, visibility beyond the next two quarters is limited, and projections for 2027 are not yet feasible. The company's short-term growth outlook for the following six months is under pressure due to the persisting cancellation trend.

data.title: The Inner Circle acknowledges Kenneth Spells as an Inner Circle Lifetime for his contributions in Educational Leadership
data.description: In Raeford, NC on April 23, 2026, Kenneth Spells is recognized as an Inner Circle Lifetime member for his achievements in Educational Leadership. Known for his dedication and impact, Spells has had a remarkable career in the field. His contributions have been highlighted in The Inner Circle, showcasing his significant accomplishments.

Stone is celebrated for her valuable contributions as a Florist, Business Owner, and Author, who consistently inspires her community through her work and dedication.

data.title: NCICU Receives $210K Grant from The Cannon Foundation to Streamline Student Transfer Pathways
data.description: On April 23, 2026, in Raleigh, North Carolina, the North Carolina Independent Colleges and Universities (NCICU) received a generous $210,000 grant from The Cannon Foundation. The grant is intended to assist the NCICU Transfer Pathways project. This funding will support the development of first-year software and related initiatives aimed at enhancing educational pathways within the NCICU network.

data.description: Merchant McIntyre (MM) has been honored as a leading firm in the lobbying industry, having been acknowledged as a top lobbying firm by Bloomberg Government. In the 2025 report, MM was recognized as a "standout firm", further solidifying its position of excellence. This acknowledgment showcases the firm's exceptional performance and distinguishes it among its peers.

data.title: IBM Stock Slides Despite Q1 Earnings Beat As Software's AI Disruption Fears Return
data.description: IBM's impressive Q1 earnings were overshadowed by concerns about the impact of AI on the software market, causing their stock to decline on Wall Street. Despite beating expectations, IBM faced apprehension with their shares sliding as investors remained wary of the disruption AI could bring. The market reaction reflected uncertainties about IBM's ability to navigate the changing landscape, leading to a drop in their stock price despite the positive earnings report. This signals a growing unease among investors regarding the challenges posed by AI in the software sector.

data.title: Nasdaq Hikes Dividend After Strong Quarter
data.description: Nasdaq Inc. (NASDAQ: NDAQ) saw a rise in shares on Thursday as the exchange operator announced their first-quarter 2026 results, surpassing expectations. Revenue grew by 14% year over year to $1.407 billion, exceeding the analyst consensus of $1.372 billion. Adjusted earnings per share were reported at 96 cents, higher than the estimated 93 cents. Annualized recurring revenue also increased by 13% to $3.2 billion. Solutions revenue went up by 14% to $1.08 billion, while Market Services net revenue grew by 13% to $317 million. Financial Technology revenue saw a 20% increase to $517 million, and Index revenue rose by 14% to $220 million. Operating cash flow for the quarter reached $689 million, with cash and equivalents at $515 million as of March 31, 2026. Nasdaq returned $153 million to shareholders through dividends and repurchased $548 million of common stock. The company also raised its quarterly dividend.
